title: Just-this-room awareness
subtitle: A five-minute bedtime meditation for grounding attention in the present room.
excerpt: A five-minute meditation for bedtime. The instruction is to place attention on the room itself: what is here, what is quiet, what is real in this moment. Useful when the mind is running lists of what isn't here yet.
sourceNotes: Present-moment awareness practice draws on the secular mindfulness tradition, widely adapted from Buddhist contemplative practice through Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR, Jon Kabat-Zinn, 1980s). The bedtime grounding version here is shaped for Day 5 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use in bed when the mind is running tomorrow's list and you want to redirect attention to what is real in this room right now.

content.text: A five-minute meditation for when the mind is elsewhere. The instruction is narrow: place attention on this room, this moment, nothing further forward than the ceiling above you.
Lie in bed. Eyes closed or soft. Three slow breaths to signal you are stopping.
Notice the sounds in the room. Not sounds from outside, not thoughts about sounds — just what is audible right now. The hum of something, the quiet. Let the sounds be there without analysis.
Notice the temperature of the air on your face. Cool or warm. Notice the weight of the covers. The pillow under your head. These are facts about this specific moment in this specific room.
When a thought about tomorrow arrives (a task, a conversation, a worry) let it pass without following it. Return to what is real in the room. The sound, the temperature, the weight of the bed.
Stay with the room for five minutes, or until sleep arrives.
If you are using this as a standalone practice, take three deeper breaths and open your eyes when you are ready.

Present-moment awareness practice draws on the secular mindfulness tradition, widely adapted from Buddhist contemplative practice through the Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR) programme developed by Jon Kabat-Zinn in the 1980s. The bedtime grounding version here is shaped for Day 5 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
